name: Ovarian Cancer - Oophorectomy/Hysterectomy Procedure Query
description: **Eighth Procedure-resource phenotype.** Tests ability to find ovarian cancer patients via surgical procedures: oophorectomy (ovary removal) and hysterectomy (uterus removal, often combined as TAH-BSO for ovarian cancer staging).
prompt: Find patients who have undergone oophorectomy or hysterectomy procedures.

prompts.broad: Find Procedure resources for oophorectomy (ovary removal) or hysterectomy (uterus removal) — the surgical procedures used for ovarian cancer staging and treatment, often performed together as total abdominal hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (TAH-BSO).
prompts.expert: I need to identify ovarian cancer patients via surgical procedures documented in their record. Search Procedure resources for oophorectomy (removal of one or both ovaries) and hysterectomy (removal of the uterus), both coded in SNOMED CT. For ovarian cancer staging, these procedures are often performed together as a total abdominal hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, so including both procedure codes ensures complete capture. Note that hysterectomy is also performed for other gynecologic indications such as uterine fibroids, endometriosis, or cervical cancer, so this procedure cohort will include some patients without ovarian cancer.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with oophorectomy or hysterectomy. **Eighth Procedure-resource phenotype.** First gynecologic-surgical procedure.
